spec_match.rationale: Uses real anchors with `scroll-target-group: auto` and `:target-current` — exactly the carousel pattern the spec discussion calls out.

interactive.rationale: Zero interactive markers (<button>, <input>, etc.) — this is a static CSS demo with scroll behaviour. The site invariant 7 explicitly forbids static cards.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says anchors become scroll markers and :target-current highlights — that's CSS behaviour, but the page has no interactivity beyond default browser scroll.

edge_cases.rationale: Nothing to interact with means nothing to fail with — no demonstration of multiple groups, keyboard nav, screen reader behaviour.

design_system.rationale: Variables only — but the lack of interactive widgets makes design system compliance less meaningful.

openQuestions.title: Add interactivity per invariant 7
openQuestions.detail: The site's invariant 7 (CLAUDE.md) explicitly forbids static demos. Add at minimum: prev/next buttons that drive scrollIntoView, a toggle to switch scroll-target-group between auto/none so the visitor sees the with/without state, and a live readout of which anchor is :target-current.

openQuestions.title: Show keyboard navigation
openQuestions.detail: Carousel a11y is hard; show that anchors give tab/arrow navigation for free vs the ::scroll-marker pseudo-element approach which has focus limitations.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate multiple groups
openQuestions.detail: Two carousels on the same page — does each have its own :target-current? Show the scoping rules.

summary: Conceptually sound (real anchors as markers) but ships as a static CSS-only card — invariant 7 fail. Needs prev/next controls, auto/none toggle, and a live :target-current readout to clear the bar.
